lines: The book is a child's copybook, half sums and half field chores. The later pages are all about the wolves beyond the north fence.
"Father says no meat over the fence. Road wolves remember a hand better than saints remember prayers. I did it anyway. Gray took the fat from my palm and did not bite."
"The little ones come when I whistle twice and sit for marrow bones. They hate red cloth. Father says I am teaching teeth to beg. I think I am teaching them our smell."
The last written page is only a feed tally: two marrow bones, one fat strip. Under it, the child wrote, DO NOT TELL FATHER.
